intro: Minuartia rosei, also known as Rosy-cupped Sandwort, is a low-growing, mat-forming perennial plant native to high-altitude alpine meadows in the western Himalayas. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, gastrointestinal disorders, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Minuartia rosei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Minuartia rosei is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin and wound healing applications.
